tax: Tufa Column is placed at species rank beneath Tufakin (Argillic order, Earth Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a hollow vertical calcite body, pressurised lime-water jets, and a coral-like pore surface. It is separated from its sibling the Calcite Crust Elemental by its vertical hollow-column form and jetting jets versus the Crust's flat spreading sheet; the two share the genus body-plan but never the flat spreading. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: precipitated calcite tufa grown around a pressurised seep channel.
ap: Tufa Column stands 0.8–1.6 m tall hollow column. Its silhouette is an upright branching tufa tower with pore-covered skin. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: a hollow interior that rings dully when struck, unlike solid limestone; thin jets of lime-water sprayed from pores on approach, 1–2 m range; and a white lime encrustation on the floor and walls for two paces around it. It moves with a slow rocking advance on a spreading calcite base, and shows none; milk-white body. Even after dispersal it can be identified from a lime-crust trail and hollow fractured tufa shards where it walked, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Tufa Column persists in active tufa terraces, lime-seep cascades, and hot-spring margins of the mid tiers where calcium-rich water jets and deposits. It does not breed; it forms where earth-pressure drives a calcium-supersaturated seep through a calcite nucleus, growing it hollow and mobile, and recurs whenever the seep re-pressurises and the fractured column regrows from the same nucleus. It sustains its mass on hydraulic pressure in calcium-supersaturated seep water, drawing in dissolved calcium drawn from seep to grow and repair. Range is bounded by the pressurised seep zone: outside that band the body dries, stops jetting, and sets inert. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a tufa terrace that grows against the seep-flow, adding height rather than breadth, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Tufa Column is roused by approach within 3 m or any impact on its column and otherwise lies a still tufa formation indistinguishable from natural travertine. It claims a single tufa terrace and answers intrusion with jetting lime-water into eyes and gear, then advancing to force the intruder off the terrace. Toward grey-folk it is slow but caustic; survey crews report that Wall-Wardens wear visors on any terrace growing against the seep-flow. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Tufa Column: a body of precipitated calcite tufa grown around a pressurised seep channel; immune to acid (the lime neutralises it) and to crushing, which it regrows; and a defining weakness — strong acid in quantity, which dissolves calcite faster than it can deposit. Its senses run through the medium itself: hydraulic-pressure sensing through its seep channel; blind to still dry objects. It tolerates heat, mineral seep, and darkness but fails at loss of seep pressure and prolonged dryness. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the hollow column form and white lime encrustation, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Tufa Column are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it jets pressurised lime-water to blind and burn; it advances its base to push prey from its terrace; it regrows fractured sections from the same seep nucleus; and it seals cracks by depositing calcite, slowly armour-plating itself. Each is a direct expression of hydraulic pressure driving calcium supersaturation through a hollow calcite body, so it cannot, for instance, move fast, cross dry ground, or survive strong acid. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Tufa Column matters because it makes active tufa terraces a blinding-jet hazard, and Wall-Wardens now visor-up whenever a terrace grows against its own flow. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a caustic-jet hazard logged with active tufa terraces, and its presence reshapes routes through the lime-seep terraces, now approached with visors and always crossed above the active deposition line. A tufa formation growing against its seep-flow, adding height rather than breadth, marks an animating column.
g: Tufa Column (Calcarius tubulans) is a chalky calcareous elemental that grows as a hollow tufa column and jets pressurised lime-water through pores. Documented from the active tufa terraces and lime-seep cascades of the mid tiers, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a hollow calcite column riddled with seep-channels and lime-encrusted pores — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. It is the hollow-bodied chalk kin: where the Calcite Crust spreads flat, the Tufa Column grows vertical and jets. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a fractured hollow column and a spreading white lime film on the floor and no carcass.
